_comment: LISTENING PROBE, not a demo. MOTION is pinned at 1.0 (maximum scatter) for the whole render while SIZE steps through four values. Question it answers: is grain LENGTH the missing lever for 'fog', or is the position-scatter ceiling the thing holding it back? Stale as of 2026-07-21: this probe predates the ranges-opening plan, which already raised kScatterPosFrac from 0.25 to 1.0 (spec 2026-07-21) -- MOTION now scatters across the WHOLE buffer, not a quarter of it. MOTION still changes neither grain length nor density (position, pan, spawn timing and octave only), so this probe's SIZE-vs-MOTION question stands, just against the wider scatter. Synth silenced throughout.

init._comment: part A silenced: zero base AND LEVEL modulation off
MOTION pinned at maximum scatter, unmodulated, for the entire render
SIZE parked and unmodulated too, so the steps below are the only thing moving it

events._comment: 35-42s: SIZE 1.0 -> 42 s (was 2 s under M5a; the SIZE ceiling was raised, spec 2026-07-21). The content clamp is gone too -- read_linear folds, so this is not a single 42 s window but the ~8 s of content read in a loop under one long window, not a hard clamp back to content length.
